component.name: Form
component.description: React Hook Form integration components providing form context, field wiring, labels, descriptions, and validation messages.

useCases: Wiring form inputs to validation state with automatic ARIA attributes
Displaying field-level error messages and descriptions
Creating consistent form layouts with label, control, description, and message slots

requiredProps.name: FormField.name
requiredProps.description: The field path in the form schema, passed to react-hook-form Controller

requiredProps.name: FormField.control
requiredProps.description: The form control object from useForm()

commonPatterns.name: basic-form-field
commonPatterns.description: Standard field with label, input, description, and error message
commonPatterns.composition:
<Form {...form}>
  <form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit(onSubmit)}>
    <FormField
      control={form.control}
      name="email"
      render={({ field }) => (
        <FormItem>
          <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
          <FormControl>
            <Input placeholder="you@example.com" {...field} />
          </FormControl>
          <FormDescription>We will never share your email.</FormDescription>
          <FormMessage />
        </FormItem>
      )}
    />
  </form>
</Form>

commonPatterns.name: select-field
commonPatterns.description: Form field wrapping a Select component
commonPatterns.composition:
<FormField
  control={form.control}
  name="role"
  render={({ field }) => (
    <FormItem>
      <FormLabel>Role</FormLabel>
      <FormControl>
        <Select onValueChange={field.onChange} defaultValue={field.value}>
          <SelectTrigger><SelectValue placeholder="Select a role" /></SelectTrigger>
          <SelectContent>
            <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem value="user">User</SelectItem>
          </SelectContent>
        </Select>
      </FormControl>
      <FormMessage />
    </FormItem>
  )}
/>

antiPatterns.scenario: Using FormItem, FormLabel, or FormMessage outside of a FormField
antiPatterns.reason: These components depend on FormFieldContext and FormItemContext provided by FormField and FormItem
antiPatterns.alternative: Always nest within FormField > FormItem hierarchy

antiPatterns.scenario: Using native form elements without wrapping in FormControl
antiPatterns.reason: FormControl applies aria-describedby, aria-invalid, and id attributes via Radix Slot
antiPatterns.alternative: Always wrap the input element with FormControl to get automatic ARIA wiring

antiPatterns.scenario: Placing FormMessage before FormControl in the DOM
antiPatterns.reason: Error messages should appear after the input for natural reading order and screen reader flow
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep the order: FormLabel, FormControl, FormDescription, FormMessage

slots.provider: Form (FormProvider) wraps the entire form and provides react-hook-form context
slots.field: FormField connects a named field to react-hook-form Controller
slots.item: FormItem provides a unique id context and grid layout for a single field
slots.label: FormLabel renders a Label linked to the field input via htmlFor
slots.control: FormControl applies ARIA attributes to the child input via Radix Slot
slots.description: FormDescription renders helper text linked via aria-describedby
slots.message: FormMessage renders validation error text or custom children

parentConstraints: Form (FormProvider) must wrap all FormField components

interactions.validation: Error state propagates from react-hook-form; FormLabel turns destructive color, FormMessage displays error text
interactions.submit: Form submission handled by react-hook-form handleSubmit passed to the native form element

responsive.mobile: FormItem uses grid gap-2 layout; fields stack naturally
responsive.desktop: Same grid layout; parent form can use multi-column grids

accessibility.role: FormControl renders as a Radix Slot, passing role from the child input
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Standard form navigation via Tab; submit via Enter
accessibility.screenReader: FormLabel linked via htmlFor; FormDescription linked via aria-describedby; errors announced via aria-describedby and aria-invalid
accessibility.focusManagement: No custom focus management; relies on native form behavior and react-hook-form

rules: Form is a re-export of react-hook-form FormProvider
FormField wraps react-hook-form Controller and provides field context
FormItem generates a unique id used to derive formItemId, formDescriptionId, and formMessageId
FormControl uses Radix Slot to merge ARIA props onto the child element without adding a wrapper
FormLabel applies data-error attribute and destructive-foreground color when the field has an error
FormMessage renders nothing when there is no error and no children
useFormField hook is exported for building custom form-connected components
alternatives: Plain Input with manual validation for simple non-form cases

aiHints.context: Use for any form with validation. Requires react-hook-form as a peer dependency. Always follow the nesting order: Form > FormField > FormItem > [FormLabel, FormControl > Input, FormDescription, FormMessage]. The Form component is just FormProvider — pass the spread form methods to it.
